tweets.full_text:
🚨 Bhutan (@gmcbhutan) launches the world’s first Solana-backed digital nomad visa. 🇧🇹

Applicants must purchase $10,000 worth of TER - tokenized gold on Solana - plus a $2,800 application fee, allowing them to stay up to 36 months in Bhutan. The $10,000 is refundable at the end of the visa term, introducing a new model that connects real-world residency with on-chain assets.

🚨 Bhutan (@gmcbhutan) launches the world’s first Solana-backed digital nomad visa. 🇧🇹

Applicants must purchase $10,000 worth of TER - tokenized gold on Solana - plus a $2,800 application fee, allowing them to stay up to 36 months in Bhutan. The $10,000 is refundable at the end of the visa term, introducing a new model that connects real-world residency with on-chain assets.

legacy.full_text:
You can now pay your bills in USDC, start a market on any trending topic, and give an AI agent the keys to a DAO. All on Solana.

Here’s everything that happened this week:

📰 Headline News

- Lightspeed, crypto's first institutional investor relations platform by @Blockworks https://t.co/wW14Jdljgn

user_mentions.name: Realms - The Home of Solana DAOs

result.text:
You can now pay your bills in USDC, start a market on any trending topic, and give an AI agent the keys to a DAO. All on Solana.

Here’s everything that happened this week:

📰 Headline News

- Lightspeed, crypto's first institutional investor relations platform by @Blockworks and Solana went live

- @altitude launched Altitude Bill Pay enabling users to pay bills in USDC

@Zora launched on Solana with Attention Markets, letting users back what the internet pays attention to

📰 Launches

- @RealmsDAOs shipped Realms MCP giving AI agents full DAO governance powers

- @dflow launched DFlow MCP for Solana trading

- @sponge_wallet debuted a financial stack for AI agents

- @metaplex teased its new unified platform

- @encifherio shipped privacy-focused cross-chain bridge with Encrypt V2

- @asgardfi released Credit Backed Positions for institutional credit

- @Inframarkets debuted the first-ever energy prediction market

- @SuperteamUKR officially landed in Ukraine

- @omnipair beta went live, introducing borrowing against memecoins

- @JupiterExchange Lend activated native staking as collateral

- @heydotlol debuted a new social network for humans and AI agents, powered by @x402

- @baysemarkets integrated Solana deposits and withdrawals

- @ycombinator is heading to @mtndao for live office hours

- @WAVEDOTFUN went live with zero-fee prediction markets

- @pumpspotlight extended the Build in Public Hackathon deadline to February 25

- @CudisWellness announced its Sporty Series ring pre launch on Kickstarter

- @MiloOnChains introduced an AI portfolio manager for Clawbot

📰 Milestones

- @helium hit ATH with 3.4M daily users and 124K active Mobile Hotspots

- @onrefinance surpassed $100M AUM

- @phantom named to the Forbes Fintech 50 list for 2026

This week's cover art by @Groowut 🔥

tweets.full_text:
I will STOP USING Meteora if they’re actually getting exposed by Zach

But honestly I can’t imagine it

People remember Libra and think it was Meteora

Here’s why I don’t think so:

If we look back at Libra it wasn’t classic insider trading from Meteora’s side

Ben supported Kelsier with “technical support” (whatever it means)

After Kelsier sniped their own token, they used Meteora’s DLMM pools to add one-sided liquidity, depositing only LIBRA while withdrawing SOL or USD equivalents.

This avoided direct market sells (which would have crashed the price immediately) and instead let retail buy pressure absorb the dumps indirectly

What I’m struggling to see is how Meteora could use its platform for insider trading beyond simply having more information ???

I’m not close with @MeteoraAG at all, but in the few conversations I’ve had with @0xSoju, he always was someone who would turn the whole company upside down to find any potential bad actors

And I think he did that after Libra...

What are the odds you crash the company you took over after an “insider scandal” just to do the same thing again lol

Honestly they also make enough money already... it's not like we had any billion dollar launches this year where you could make millions with insider information

And why would Zach dig up some old story that we already know?

Maybe I’m naive, but I have a strong feeling CT is overhyping this and the outcome won’t even involve the very big names
tweets.name: fabiano.sol

tweets.full_text:
Everyone is speculating

But no one is actually asking how these companies could have done the “insider trading”

Here’s how I’m thinking about it:

1. Meteora

Projects often work with Meteora to launch tokens using their tek

That could theoretically give them more data (timing, CA, etc.), which might allow early sniping and exits via one-sided LP

What speaks against this is that there haven’t been many billion dollar launches recently, and it’s unlikely Zach would base this on old Libra era stories

2. Axiom

Axiom’s setup can create many wallets to buy into new meme tokens, making it look like there are far more holders than there actually are.

This can enable “speed bumping” (a type of MEV), where insiders could potentially enter and exit before whales

Of course, it looks bad but is MEV really insider trading? I doubt it

3. MEXC

MEXC has a poor reputation on CT mainly due to:

- very early token listings (often with leverage)
- user reports about withdrawals and KYC

Zach has already written about MEXC several times. The White Whale is also a donor to Zach and has a long history of issues withdrawing funds from MEXC

In general, CEXes are harder to investigate since not everything is onchain which may be where the interviews Zach mentioned come into play?

4. Pump Fun

The main way insider behavior could happen on Pump Fun would be classic pump and dump or cabal schemes

Sometimes Pump Fun featured certain memecoins, but in 2026 the volume likely wasn’t large enough to generate major insider profits.

Their bonding mechanism is onchain and monitored by multiple protocols. Scams exist, but not in a way that clearly benefits employees directly.

5. Binance

Binance often gets accused of market manipulation and involvement in the 10/10 liquidation event.

However, Zach is currently doing work involving BSC wallets, which makes this scenario less likely.

So who is it then?

Crypto Twitter is extremely good at spreading rumors, creating noise, and making things way bigger than they are...

Even Zach said he didn’t expect this to go so viral

But interestingly, almost no one is talking about the wave of perp DEXes that suddenly appeared in Q3 and Q4…

My guess: it might be a project that isn’t even being discussed yet

But we’ll see
tweets.name: fabiano.sol

legacy.description: Making a living with DeFi
